generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary actuator for axis control in the MABI MAX 100 industrial robot, interfaced with Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller for programmable position, speed, and acceleration management."
]

instance Sinumerik840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller enabling separate axis management with programmable inputs for motion control of the industrial robot's drives."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Primary position encoder mounted directly on the permanent magnet synchronous motor for real-time axis position tracking."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance GearboxPositionEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary position encoder positioned behind the gearbox for redundant position measurement and data validation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData GearboxPositionDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor monitoring the operational environment of the industrial robot for contextual data aggregation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance ForceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Force sensor measuring operational forces during robot motion for performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData ForceDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorPositionEncoder
]

instance GearboxPositionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom GearboxPositionEncoder
]

instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor
]

instance ForceDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ForceSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance DigitalShadowReferenceModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "UML-based reference model defining the structure of Digital Shadow components including data traces, aggregation, and abstraction for specific monitoring purposes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MQTTProtocolEnabler
]

instance SysMLFunctionalArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagrams representing the functional architecture of the industrial robot system for behavior modeling."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SimulinkBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink model for dynamic system behavior simulation including state machines and friction model exchanges for performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance ContextualDataTraces : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated time-series data traces from physical sensors representing operational states, position, temperature, and force metrics."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorPositionDataTransmitted, GearboxPositionDataTransmitted, TemperatureDataTransmitted, ForceDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataAggregationEnabler
]

instance DerivedMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Higher-level abstractions derived from raw sensor data including anomalies, trends, and performance indicators for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ContextualDataTraces
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AbstractionEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol implementation for real-time or near-real-time sensor data streaming from physical robot components to Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, AggregationService
]

instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML v1.7 tooling for systems engineering modeling including functional and logical architecture representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralModelingService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for behavioral simulation including state machine modeling and friction model exchanges."
    DTDFVocab:enables SimulationService
]

instance DataAggregationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing components for aggregating raw sensor data into time-series datasets and event logs for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService, AggregationService
]

instance AbstractionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulation tools and methods for transforming raw data into higher-level abstractions including derived metrics and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnalysisService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time or near-real-time data ingestion service from physical robot sensors via MQTT protocol for operational monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalInsight, DataVisualizationInsight
]

instance AggregationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated processing service converting raw sensor data into structured contextual data traces and derived metrics."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessAnalysisInsight
]

instance BehavioralModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service using SysML state machines and MontiArc automata for simulating system behavior and enabling what-if analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance SimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation service using Simulink for validating system behavior and exchanging friction models for performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight
]

instance AnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service for analyzing derived metrics and performance indicators to generate insights."
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessAnalysisInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OperationalInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time operational insights derived from continuous sensor data monitoring including position, velocity, and force metrics."
]

instance DataVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of contextual data traces through dashboard interfaces for diagnostic and analytical purposes."
]

instance ProcessAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Derived insights from processed data including performance trends, operational patterns, and potential inefficiencies."
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of deviations from expected system behavior through behavioral modeling and simulation comparisons."
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insights generated from simulation-based analysis on energy efficiency, wear patterns, and operational limits."
]

// Environment
instance ManufacturingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Industrial manufacturing environment containing the MABI MAX 100 robot system and its associated physical components."
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, Sinumerik840DslController, MotorPositionEncoder, GearboxPositionEncoder, EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor, ForceSensor
]

instance DigitalShadowSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital Shadow system environment containing all modeling components, data processing services, and simulation tools."
    base:contains DigitalShadowReferenceModel, SysMLFunctionalArchitecture, SimulinkBehaviorModel, ContextualDataTraces, DerivedMetricsData, MQTTProtocolEnabler, SysMLEnabler, SimulinkEnabler, DataAggregationEnabler, AbstractionEnabler, MonitoringService, AggregationService, BehavioralModelingService, SimulationService, AnalysisService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in the described Digital Twin system is **not explicitly detailed** in the provided documents. However, the distinction between **digital shadows** (passive, one-way data flow) and **Digital Twins** (active, bidirectional) implies that a DT could include:

1. **Control Flows**:
   - **Not in Document** (no mention of command types, control message formats, or feedback loops).

2. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Not in Document** (no specifics on security protocols, access controls, or validation rules for commands).

3. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Not in Document** (no description of closed-loop mechanisms or conflict resolution strategies).

4. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - **Not in Document** (no details on acknowledgment protocols or error handling).

The documents focus primarily on **digital shadows** ([32], [56]) and **modeling methodologies** ([17], [28]), with no explicit coverage of active control interactions from the DT to physical systems.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents does not explicitly detail synchronization frequencies, time scales, or latency requirements between physical and digital components. However, the Digital Shadow Reference Model emphasizes a **one-way data flow** from physical objects to digital representations, where data traces are aggregated and processed in real-time to form a **live, data-driven representation** of the system. While the documents reference a **purpose-driven design** for Digital Shadows (e.g., injection molding use cases refined in [42]), no specific temporal granularity (e.g., millisecond-level or high-frequency sensor data) is mentioned for the primary system discussed. The system relies on **streamed sensor data** (e.g., via MQTT in Section IV.A) but does not specify explicit synchronization protocols or time-scale constraints for the Digital Twin itself. Traceability of data traces is maintained via transitive associations, ensuring temporal consistency in processing components, but no quantitative temporal metrics (e.g., update intervals, latency bounds) are provided for the Digital Twin system beyond the passive aggregation of data traces in the Digital Shadow framework."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is orchestrated through a **reference model-based concretization process**, where the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** [Document 2] serves as the foundational architecture. This model defines a **component-based structure** comprising:
   - **Data Trace Sources** (physical system inputs),
   - **Processing Components** (simulation/data calculation models, e.g., MathWorks Simulink [28] or general-purpose languages like C++/Java),
   - **Metadata Aggregation** for context-aware processing,
   - **Output Data Traces** forming the digital shadow.
   The **conforming model** (a concrete realization) incorporates **architecture patterns** such as **component & connector perspectives** (e.g., ACME [23], AADL [24], or SysML IBDs [17]), where components are described with **interfaces**, **interconnections**, and **composition rules**. The **Processing component** acts as the **central coordination hub**, consuming input traces, applying **data calculation or simulation models**, and producing output traces with **fine-grained traceability** (transitive links to input traces and models). Resource management is implied through the **purpose-driven design** of each shadow instance, where **metadata** supports specialized processing (e.g., filtering, normalization). No explicit mention of **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or **real-time coordination protocols** (e.g., DDS, MQTT) is provided, but the **traceability framework** ensures system-wide consistency. The **SMArDT methodology** [Document 1] suggests alignment with **model-based systems engineering (MBSE)** practices for integrating heterogeneous stakeholders and perspectives."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The transition between these stages is governed by **methodologies such as RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, or **SMArDT**, which ensure systematic progression from conceptual design to implementation. **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, and SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** are employed to formalize component-connector perspectives, including interfaces, interconnections, and composition, while **SysML state machines (v1.7) or MontiArc Automaton** define behavioral dynamics. **Simulation tools (e.g., MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose languages like C++/Java)** integrate detailed behavioral modeling. Lifecycle governance involves **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool model coordination**, ensuring alignment across disciplines. Transition management relies on **formalized abstraction hierarchies** and **toolchain interoperability**, with governance frameworks implicitly derived from **MBSE best practices** (e.g., iterative refinement, validation at each layer).
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The system addresses **fidelity and validity** through **sensor arrangement optimizations**, such as dual encoders, which reduce **backlash and positional uncertainty**. Validation is implicitly defined by the **solution scope**—comparing **simulated data versus measured data**—to quantify deviations and ensure model accuracy. Uncertainty sources are mitigated via hardware redundancy (e.g., encoders), though explicit **error detection/correction mechanisms** or **quality assurance processes** (e.g., calibration protocols, statistical validation methods) are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical network infrastructure for this Digital Twin system relies on **MQTT protocol** for streaming sensor data from physical objects to the digital representation. The communication topology is not explicitly detailed, but the system assumes a **client-server architecture** (e.g., using Mosquitto as an MQTT broker, as referenced in [52]). Bandwidth and latency requirements are not specified, though the context implies real-time or near-real-time data transmission. Reliability mechanisms are not mentioned, and security protocols for network communication are absent from the provided documents. The system does not describe bidirectional control flows, focusing instead on passive data aggregation."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The deployment infrastructure for this system is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. The system references the use of **MBSE tools** (e.g., SysML, Simulink) and **dashboard platforms** (e.g., ThingsBoard, implied by [53]) for visualization and analysis, but no specific hosting platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise) are mentioned. Computational and storage requirements, scalability mechanisms, or deployment models (e.g., hybrid) are not described. Availability or reliability assurances are not provided."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration in the Digital Twin system is facilitated through **heterogeneous modeling notations and tools**, as outlined in the **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** framework [Document 1]. The system integrates with external systems via:
   - **Data Exchange Protocols**: Implicitly supported by the **one-way data flow** from physical systems to digital shadows, though no specific protocols (e.g., OPC UA, REST APIs) are mentioned.
   - **Interoperability Standards**: The use of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like SysML, AADL, or MontiArc [25]–[27] enables **semantic interoperability** between components and external systems. SysML, for example, supports **state machines** and **interfaces** for defining connectors to external tools (e.g., Simulink [28]).
   - **Dependencies on External Systems**: The **Processing component** may rely on external **simulation tools** (e.g., Simulink) or **general-purpose languages** (e.g., C++, Java) for computational tasks, indicating **tight coupling** with external software ecosystems.
   - **Integration Patterns**: The **conforming model** [Document 2] allows for **customization of data trace sources**, implying that external systems (e.g., IoT platforms, ERP systems) can be integrated as long as they provide the required **data traces**. The **purpose-driven design** [42] suggests that integration patterns are tailored to specific use cases (e.g., injection molding), but no standardized **enterprise integration patterns** (e.g., ESB, API gateways) are described. The **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., RFLP, OOSEM) emphasize **stakeholder integration**, ensuring alignment with external system requirements."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
Not in Document

]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents incorporates **physical safety mechanisms** for robotic systems, specifically referencing the **safety cell** of the MABI robot (document [44]) to restrict its operational workspace to a predefined range of [-90°, 90°], ensuring collision avoidance and operator safety. No explicit **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., encryption, intrusion detection, or access control frameworks) are mentioned in the documents.

The **RAMI 4.0** reference architecture (document [49]) is referenced in broader industry discussions but does not detail **security measures** (e.g., authentication, threat mitigation, or fail-safe mechanisms) for the described Digital Twin system. Similarly, **ThingsBoard** (document [53]) is not referenced in the provided documents, and thus no IoT platform-specific security considerations (e.g., MQTT protocol security, role-based access control) can be extracted.

The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (documents [46], [47], [50]) provides a **semantic framework** for asset modeling but does not explicitly address **data governance, privacy compliance (e.g., GDPR), or consent management** in the provided context. The **Functional Mockup Interface (FMI)** (document [51]) focuses on **model exchange** for simulation but lacks security or safety specifications.

No documentation explicitly outlines **data anonymization, sharing agreements, or compliance with privacy regulations** (e.g., anonymization techniques, pseudonymization, or legal frameworks). The provided sources emphasize **technical implementation** (e.g., SysML, Simulink, friction modeling) and **automation pyramid integration** (document [42]) but omit **cyber-physical security controls** or **risk assessment methodologies** for the Digital Twin system.
"
]
